questions.question:
Let \( \text{R (A, B, C, D)} \) be a relational schema with the following functional dependencies: 

\( \text{A} \rightarrow \text{B,  B} \rightarrow \text{C} \) 

\( \text{C} \rightarrow \text{D} \) and \( \text{D} \rightarrow \text{B} \)

The decomposition of \( \text{R} \) into

\( \text{(A, B), (B, C), (B, D)} \)



Databases: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section B | Question: 49
options: gives a lossless join, and is dependency preserving
gives a lossless join, but is not dependency preserving
does not give a lossless join, but is dependency preserving
does not give a lossless join and is not dependency preserving
questions.correct_answer: gives a lossless join, and is dependency preserving
